Location: Washington, District of Columbia, United States
Headline: Chairman and CEO at Bechtel Corporation
Summary:
Growing up on Bechtel projects across the globe, I saw firsthand why my father loved to say, “give Bechtel people a challenge, and they’ll make history.” Today, I’m proud to lead this company as we help customers solve for tomorrow’s infrastructure, energy, security, and resource needs.

My favorite days as CEO are spent walking job sites with our people. LinkedIn gives me the chance to share a glimpse of what Bechtel’s incredible people do and the positive impact they have. 

I’ll also occasionally use this space to write about innovation, diversity, and other ways I believe we can do our part to design and build a cleaner, safer, more sustainable, and more prosperous future for everyone.

My other goal is to use LinkedIn to listen. Even with the help of a few colleagues, I won’t have time to respond to nearly as many of your comments as I would like. I’ll read as much as I can and do my best to chime in when I have something to add.

#WeAreBechtel

Headline: SAP Consultant | Implementation & Training Specialist | User-Adoption and Change Leader
Summary:
I help organizations translate complex SAP implementations into meaningful user adoption, partnering with clients such as Levi’s, Costco, Warner Bros., Ingram Micro, Applied Materials, Kiewit, and Cigna.

With a background in Organizational Change Management and Training, I design programs that make large-scale change understandable, practical, and sustainable. Because I’m most energized when helping people feel capable in the face of new systems and processes, my approach blends structure with soft skills like curiosity and empathy.

I believe successful adoption depends as much on clear communication and well-designed learning as it does on technical delivery. Whether in the boardroom or the warehouse, I help people find clarity and confidence in moments of change.

Headline: Executive Chairman & Founder at Clayco
Summary:
Founder and Executive Chairman of Clayco, one of the leading design-build firms in the United States.
Bob Clark founded Clayco in 1984 in the St. Louis area as a two-person operation. Today, the company has more than 4,000 employees and generated over $8.1 billion in U.S. revenue in 2025.

As Executive Chairman, Bob focuses on solving customers’ most complex problems, developing talent, and shaping long-term strategy.

In 2021 and 2022, Bob served as the United States Commissioner General for the USA Pavilion at Expo 2020 Dubai. Appointed by the White House, he was the senior U.S. government representative and head of delegation. The USA Pavilion highlighted American values, innovation, and cultural diversity while advancing U.S. positions on global priorities, including climate, space, food security, health, and technology.

About Clayco

Clayco is a full-service, turnkey real estate, architecture, engineering, design-build, and construction firm. The company specializes in the art and science of building, delivering fast-track, efficient solutions across North America for mission-critical, commercial, institutional, industrial, and residential projects.

Business Impact

• Consistently ranked among the top firms for both design and construction by ENR, Midwest Real Estate News, and other industry publications.

• Recognized for sustainability and LEED building design, business best practices, and as one of the best places to work by ENR, SLCCC, and the St. Louis Business Journal.

• A leader in safety management with an EMR of 0.39 across its jobsite workforce.

Community Impact

• Active support of medical research focused on improving outcomes for patients with rare and genetic diseases. Bob is a former Trustee of the University of Chicago Medical Center, a board member of the Central Institute for the Deaf, and a member of the Scientific Advisory Council for Washington University School of Medicine in St. Louis.

• BobClarkBeyond is a community effort focused on mentorship, impact, adventure, and inspiration, with an emphasis on art, science, education, economic development, and good government.

• Clayco welcomes talent from diverse backgrounds and supports global companies investing in the United States. Forty-six languages are spoken by Clayco team members.

Headline: Chairman and Chief Executive Officer at Suffolk
Summary:
John F. Fish is the Chairman and CEO of Suffolk, a national contractor and real estate enterprise that invests, innovates and builds. Under his vision and leadership, Suffolk has grown into one of the leading privately held builders in the country. With over $10 billion in annual revenue, Suffolk is currently ranked on the Engineering News-Record national list of Top Contractors.
 
Mr. Fish is a fixture on numerous Boards focused on economic stability and real estate industry growth. He is the Chair of the Real Estate Roundtable, a non-partisan organization that convenes government, business and communities to establish more equitable policies for economic growth. He is the founding member and current Director of the Massachusetts Competitive Partnership (MACP) and former Chairman of the Board of the Federal Reserve Bank of Boston. In New York City, Mr. Fish is a Director of the Real Estate Board of New York and a member of the Partnership for New York City, and he sits on the National Business Roundtable. Mr. Fish serves as Chair of Brigham and Women’s Hospital and sits on the Executive Committee of Mass General Brigham. He also serves on the Board of the Feinstein Institutes for Medical Research at Northwell Health. He is the Chair of the Board at Boston College, his second time serving that role and first non-alum to fulfill that role. Mr. Fish is also on the Board of Overseers for Home Base. In 2009, Mr. Fish founded Scholar Athletes and led the program for more than ten years, improving the lives of thousands of inner-city high school students by leveraging the proven link between school athletics and academics.
 
Mr. Fish is a graduate of Bowdoin College in Brunswick, Maine with a Bachelor’s degree in Political Science. He received an honorary doctorate of Engineering Technology degree from Wentworth Institute of Technology, is the recipient of an honorary degree from Regis College, and received an honorary degree in Business Administration from Curry College.

Summary:

Headline: VDC Coordinator at Kiewit | Mission Critical Data Center | Master’s in Construction Management | Proven track record of delivering innovative solutions
Summary:
The world of construction is where my heart truly lies—it's an industry where vision meets reality every day. I’ve always believed that “you can dream, create, design, and build the most wonderful place in the world,” but it takes dedicated people to make those dreams come alive. With a Master's degree in Construction Management from The University of Texas at Arlington and a Bachelor’s degree in Architecture from the University of Mumbai, I have cultivated both technical knowledge and practical skills throughout my career.

As a VDC Coordinator at Kiewit since April 2024, I've been part of transformative projects that not only push boundaries but also prioritize sustainability. Previously as a BIM Manager at Yates Construction for over a year, I led initiatives to streamline processes that ultimately saved time across multiple projects—talk about efficiency! My time at Superior Concrete Products involved creating detailed renders using Lumion that helped clarify complex concepts for clients—a game changer when it came to project communication.

Before entering the professional realm full-time, I had the privilege of working as an intern with Supreme International Construction where I learned the ropes of project management—balancing deadlines while working alongside seasoned professionals was an invaluable experience! Notably during my research assistantship at UTA, I performed soil tests on highway samples which deepened my understanding of material properties crucial for successful project execution.

When I’m not delving into construction technology or sustainability practices (I’m also pursuing certifications related to AI and machine learning), you’ll find me engaging with community initiatives or sharing insights on sustainable building design as a Fitwel Ambassador. These experiences ground my professional journey while reminding me why I love this field so much: it’s about improving lives through better spaces.

If you’re interested in discussing innovative building technology or sustainable practices—or just want to chat about the latest trends—I’d love to connect! Feel free to reach out via email.

Skills: 
- Building Information Modeling (BIM)
- Sustainable Building Design
- Project Management
- Building Technology
- Architectural Design & Drafting

Headline: Senior Director Continuous Improvement | Sr Program Manager | Professor of Cybersecurity IT & Management | Author | Master Black Belt LSS | Scaled Agile SPC6 | International Agile Coaching ICP-ACC |
Summary:
**Executive Summary**

Dr. Raed Sbeit is a globally recognized authority in network technology, cybersecurity, and management. As a dynamic leader and strategic visionary, he has consistently delivered transformative results in high-stakes environments. Holding a Doctorate in Engineering Management and an MS in Software Engineering from Southern Methodist University (SMU), Dr. Sbeit blends robust academic credentials with unparalleled professional acumen.

At AT&T, Dr. Sbeit excelled as the Solution Train Engineer (STE), driving program alignment, optimizing value delivery, and managing complex cross-team dependencies. His strategic leadership was instrumental in scaling Charles Schwab’s Portfolio Accounting & Management applications, seamlessly integrating $54 trillion in assets post-merger, and exponentially increasing system capacity.

At Bechtel National Program Management Office (Mashroat), Dr. Sbeit led a nationwide Lean Six Sigma implementation, significantly enhancing operational efficiency across 47 entities in Saudi Arabia. His tenure at Verizon showcased his ability to lead large-scale transformations, where he spearheaded the Business Network Transformation, improving throughput by 10% for a 4000+ Network Systems organization through Scaled Agile Framework (SAFe) methodologies.

Founder of the North Texas Leadership Development Academy, Dr. Sbeit has trained and certified over 1000 professionals from Fortune 500 companies in Lean Six Sigma and Agile practices, fostering a new generation of leaders adept in process excellence and strategic innovation.

A distinguished academic, Dr. Sbeit has imparted his expertise in engineering management and cybersecurity at premier institutions such as the University of North Texas, University of Texas at Dallas, and University of Maryland Global Campus. He is an acclaimed author and active member of prestigious professional organizations, including the Industry Advisory Board of Robert Morris University and the Harvard Business Review.

Dr. Sbeit’s exceptional leadership, strategic foresight, and commitment to excellence make him an outstanding candidate for a Senior Program Manager role. His ability to drive significant business transformations, coupled with his extensive technical and managerial expertise, ensures he will be a pivotal asset to any organization.
